channel.title: The Chen-Liu Lab
channel.description: Recent content on The Chen-Liu Lab

item.description: Group Members Jingyao Chen Hongyu Liu Pengpeng Liu Postdoc Postdoc Postdoc Ping Tan Jian Wang Jing Xu Postdoc Postdoc Postdoc Qi Zhang Xuelan Chen Jiajia Du Postdoc PhD Student PhD Student Xiangyu Pan Lu Qi Xudong Wan PhD Student PhD Student PhD Student Manli Wang Yiyun Wang Zhongwang Wang PhD Student PhD Student PhD Student Baohong Wu Mengsha Zhang Jianan Zheng PhD Student PhD Student PhD Student Ailing Zhong Siqi Dai Xintong Deng PhD Student Master Student Master Student Fan Gu Ruobing Li Xiangmeng Luo Master Student Master Student Master Student Yiman Peng Bo Wang Yingjie Wang Master Student Master Student Master Student Yuyin Wang Yucen Yang Lanxin Zhang Master Student Master Student Master Student Lu Zhang Lei Zhao Master Student Master Student   Alumni Mei Chen Pengliang Chi Linjie Guo PhD Master Postdoc Xiaohang Hang Xi Huang Dan Liao PhD PhD Master Shujun Li Chao Liu Li Liu PhD Master Postdoc Lin Luo Zhenghao Lu Lei Lv Master Postdoc Postdoc Feifei Na Kaidou Shi Zhengmin Xu Postdoc Master PhD Yi Yang PhD

The Chen-Liu Laboratory Investigate cancer as a biology and a treatment target Principal Investigators                     Chong Chen, PhDPhD, University of Michigan-Ann Arbor (2009) BS, Peking University (1999)                           Yu Liu, PhDPhD, Albert Einstein College of Medicine (2008) BS, Beijing Normal University (1997) Research Interests The Chen-Liu lab aims to understand the biology of cancer and identify new therapeutic targets for treatment. We develop and apply precision cancer models, genome editing, organoid culturing, multi-omics analyses, bioinformatics, and clinical studies to build a full technique chain from basic cancer studies to clinic oncology practice.
Contact Email: chongchen AT scu.edu.cn (Prof. Chong Chen) yuliuscu AT scu.edu.cn (Prof. Yu Liu) Address: 17th Renmin Rd South, Chengdu, Sichuan Province 610065

Events 2022 April 21th Congratulations to Feifei Na, Xiangyu Pan, Jingyao Chen,Xuelan Chen and Manli Wang for their paper “KMT2C deficiency promotes small cell lung cancer metastasis through DNMT3A-mediated epigenetic reprogramming” to be published on Nature Cancer! April 13th Congratulations to Xiangyu, Jian and Linjie for their paper “Identifying a confused cell identity for esophageal squamous cell carcinoma” to be published on Signal Transduct Target Ther!

The Chen-Liu Laboratory Investigate cancer as a biology and a treatment target Principal Investigators                     Chong Chen, PhDPhD, University of Michigan-Ann Arbor (2009) BS, Peking University (1999)                           Yu Liu, PhDPhD, Albert Einstein College of Medicine (2008) BS, Beijing Normal University (1997) Research Interests The Chen-Liu lab aims to understand the biology of cancer and identify new therapeutic targets for treatment. We develop and apply precision cancer models, genome editing, organoid culturing, multi-omics analyses, bioinformatics, and clinical studies to build a full technique chain from basic cancer studies to clinic oncology practice.

Nature. 2016 Mar 24;531(7595):471-475. doi: 10.1038/nature17157. Epub 2016 Mar 16. Deletions linked to TP53 loss drive cancer through p53-independent mechanisms Liu Y, Chen C, Xu Z, Scuoppo C, Rillahan CD, Gao J, Spitzer B, Bosbach B, Kastenhuber ER, Baslan T, Ackermann S, Cheng L, Wang Q, Niu T, Schultz N, Levine RL, Mills AA, Lowe SW. Abstract Mutations disabling the TP53 tumour suppressor gene represent the most frequent events in human cancer and typically occur through a two-hit mechanism involving a missense mutation in one allele and a 'loss of heterozygosity' deletion encompassing the other.

Signal Transduct Target Ther . 2019 Apr 26;4:11. doi: 10.1038/s41392-019-0040-2. eCollection 2019. Epigenetic drug library screening identified an LSD1 inhibitor to target UTX-deficient cells for differentiation therapy Wu B, Pan X, Chen X, Chen M, Shi K, Xu J, Zheng J, Niu T, Chen C, Shuai X, Liu Y. Abstract UTX (also known as KDM6A), a histone 3 lysine 27 demethylase, is among the most frequently mutated epigenetic regulators in myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) and acute myeloid leukemia (AML).

Cancer Discov.2021 Jan;11(1):194-207. doi: 10.1158/2159-8290.CD-20-0336. Epub 2020 Sep 25. An Epigenetic Mechanism Underlying Chromosome 17p Deletion-Driven Tumorigenesis Chen M, Chen X, Li S, Pan X, Gong Y, Zheng J, Xu J, Zhao C, Zhang Q, Zhang S, Qi L, Wang Z, Shi K, Ding BS, Xue Z, Chen L, Yang S, Wang Y, Niu T, Dai L, Lowe SW, Chen C, Liu Y. Abstract Chromosome copy-number variations are a hallmark of cancer.

Cancer Cell. 2014 May 12;25(5):652-65. doi: 10.1016/j.ccr.2014.03.016. Epub 2014 May 1. MLL3 is a haploinsufficient 7q tumor suppressor in acute myeloid leukemia Chen C, Liu Y, Rappaport AR, Kitzing T, Schultz N, Zhao Z, Shroff AS, Dickins RA, Vakoc CR, Bradner JE, Stock W, LeBeau MM, Shannon KM, Kogan S, Zuber J, Lowe SW. Abstract Recurring deletions of chromosome 7 and 7q [-7/del(7q)] occur in myelodysplastic syndromes and acute myeloid leukemia (AML) and are associated with poor prognosis.

JCI Insight. 2022 Feb 8;7(3):e153768. doi: 10.1172/jci.insight.153768. Mosaic loss of chromosome Y promotes leukemogenesis and clonal hematopoiesis Zhang Q, Zhao L, Yang Y, Li S, Liu Y, Chen C. Abstract Mosaic loss of chromosome Y (mLOY) in blood cells is one of the most frequent chromosome alterations in adult males. It is strongly associated with clonal hematopoiesis, hematopoietic malignancies, and other hematopoietic and nonhematopoietic diseases. However, whether there is a causal relationship between mLOY and human diseases is unknown.

Nature cancer . 2022 April 21. doi: https://doi.org/10.1038/s43018-022-00361-6. KMT2C deficiency promotes small cell lung cancer metastasis through DNMT3A-mediated epigenetic reprogramming Feifei Na, Xiangyu Pan, Jingyao Chen, Xuelan Chen, Manli Wang, Pengliang Chi, Liting You, Lanxin Zhang, Ailing Zhong, Lei Zhao, Siqi Dai, Mengsha Zhang, Yiyun Wang, Bo Wang, Jianan Zheng, Yuying Wang, Jing Xu, Jian Wang, Baohong Wu, Mei Chen, Hongyu Liu, Jianxin Xue, Meijuan Huang, Youling Gong, Jiang Zhu, Lin Zhou, Yan Zhang, Min Yu, Panwen Tian, Mingyu Fan, Zhenghao Lu, Zhihong Xue, Yinglan Zhao, Hanshuo Yang, Chengjian Zhao, Yuan Wang, Junhong Han, Shengyong Yang, Dan Xie, Lu Chen, Qian Zhong, Musheng Zeng, Scott W.

Blood. 2020 Mar 12;135(11):826-833. doi: 10.1182/blood.2019003886. Nivolumab treatment of relapsed/refractory Epstein-Barr virus-associated hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis in adults Liu P, Pan X, Chen C, Niu T, Shuai X, Wang J, Chen X, Liu J, Guo Y, Xie L, Wu Y, Liu Y, Liu T. Abstract Epstein-Barr virus (EBV)-associated hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (EBV-HLH) is a life-threatening hyperinflammatory syndrome triggered by EBV infection. It often becomes relapsed or refractory (r/r), given that etoposide-based regimens cannot effectively clear the virus.

Signal Transduct Target Ther. 2022 Apr 13; doi: 10.1038/s41392-022-00946-8. PMID: 35418165. Identifying a confused cell identity for esophageal squamous cell carcinoma Xiangyu Pan, Jian Wang, Linjie Guo, Feifei Na, Jiajia Du, Xuelan Chen, Ailing Zhong, Lei Zhao, Lu Zhang, Mengsha Zhang, Xudong Wan, Manli Wang, Hongyu Liu, Siqi Dai, Ping Tan, Jingyao Chen, Yu Liu, Bing Hu & Chong Chen Abstract The cell identity of malignant cells and how they acquire it are fundamental for our understanding of cancer.

Pubulications #Co-first authors; *Co-corresponding authors 2022 Na F, Pan X, Chen J, Chen X, Wang M, Chi P, You L, Zhang L, Zhong A, Zhao L, Dai S, Zhang M, Wang Y, Wang B, Zheng J, Wang Y, Xu J, Wang J, Wu B, Chen M, Liu H, Xue J, Huang M, Gong Y, Zhu J, Zhou L, Zhang Y, Yu M, Tian P, Fan M, Lu Z, Xue Z, Zhao Y, Yang H, Zhao C, Wang Y, Han J, Yang S, Xie D, Chen L, Zhong Q, Zeng M, Scott W.

item.title: Chong Chen, PhD
item.description:
Professor, National Key Laboratory of Biotherapy, West China Hospital, Sichuan University. Education
BS, Peking University (1999) PhD, University of Michigan-Ann Arbor (2009) Working Experience
Postdoc, Scott Lowe laboratory, Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory & Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center (2010-2014) Professor, Sichuan University (2014-) Selective Publication

item.title: Yu Liu, PhD
item.description:
Professor, National Key Laboratory of Biotherapy, West China Hospital, Sichuan University. Education
BS, Beijing Normal University (1997) MS, Beijing Normal University (2000) PhD, Albert Einstein College of Medicine, Bronx (2008) Working Experience
Postdoc, University of Michigan (2008-2010) Postdoc, Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory & Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center (2010-2015) Professor, Sichuan University (2015-) Selective Publication
